text.title: MCP Failure Modes
text.summary: Six concrete failure modes that recur in shipped MCP servers, each paired with a detector and a fix.

antipattern.description: Stdio transport spawns a fresh subprocess per client connection — under many concurrent agents the host hits PID/fd limits.
antipattern.reason: Stdio is one-process-per-client by design. A naive deployment of stdio behind a load balancer multiplies cost linearly with client count. Detect via PID count > 50 for the server binary in `ps`. Fix: switch to Streamable HTTP for ≥2 concurrent clients; keep stdio for single-process local installs only.

antipattern.description: Client and server negotiate incompatible MCP spec versions and the server's tools simply do not appear — no exception raised.
antipattern.reason: The handshake fails silently for tool registration; agents see "no tools available" but no error log. Detect via `mcp-inspector list-tools --server X` returns empty. Fix: log the negotiated spec version on every handshake; fail-loud if no overlap; pin client and server to the same spec in CI.

antipattern.description: Streamable HTTP session held open by a load balancer with a short idle timeout drops mid-conversation; the agent gets a transport error on the next tool call.
antipattern.reason: Reverse proxies (nginx, ALB) default to 60-300 s idle timeouts; long agent runs exceed this. Detect via 504 / connection-reset on randomly-distributed long sessions. Fix: set proxy idle timeout ≥ longest expected agent turn; emit a keep-alive event every 30 s.

antipattern.description: Tool inputSchema uses `oneOf` / `anyOf` to express alternative call shapes; the LLM picks the wrong branch or skips the tool entirely.
antipattern.reason: LLM tool selectors are trained on flat schemas. Polymorphic schemas introduce ambiguity that defeats selection accuracy. Detect via eval traces showing the tool is listed but never chosen. Fix: split the polymorphic tool into N flat tools with distinct names (`db.run_query` and `db.list_tables`, not `db.action`).

antipattern.description: Remote MCP server bound on `0.0.0.0` with `auth.mode=none` because "we're inside the VPC".
antipattern.reason: VPC trust boundary leaks. The first sidecar-to-internet egress turns the server into a public tool surface. Detect via `nmap` from the public DMZ. Fix: enforce OAuth 2.1 Resource Server posture for any non-localhost bind; validate bearer against `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`.

antipattern.description: Server process crashes (unhandled exception); the next agent tool call returns a generic connection error and no alert fires.
antipattern.reason: MCP has no health-monitoring channel — the only signal is "next call failed". Without external monitoring, the outage is invisible until users notice. Detect via systemd / k8s liveness on the `ping` tool failing for ≥2 consecutive checks. Fix: wire `ping` to a process supervisor; alert on non-200.
